reason: The meeting could have been an email, the email could have been a comment, and the comment could have been better variable names.
Rewriting it in Rust will not make it faster if the bottleneck is a database query that returns ten thousand rows you then sort in memory.
That is not a bug fix. That is a load-bearing workaround with a comment saying 'TODO: fix properly' from four years ago.
Merging on Friday afternoon does not count as a deployment strategy.
The correct number of microservices is not 'one per database column.'
Disabling the failing test is not the same as fixing the thing the test was testing.
Adding more logging is not a substitute for understanding what the code is supposed to do.
The abstraction is not worth it until you actually need it the second time, and even then you should wait for the third.
Storing passwords in plaintext is not a performance optimization.
That is a regex. It will not be 'quick to update later.'
If it only works on your machine, the correct answer is to fix the code, not to ship your machine.
A ten-thousand-line file is not 'well-organized.' It is a warning.
